2605.11051.title: On Problems of Implicit Context Compression for Software Engineering Agents
2605.11051.paper_summary: LLM-based Software Engineering agents face a critical bottleneck: context length limitations cause failures on complex, long-horizon tasks. One promising solution is to encode context as continuous embeddings rather than discrete tokens, enabling denser information storage. We apply the recently proposed In-Context Autoencoder for this purpose. While the method performs well on single-shot common-knowledge and code-understanding tasks, our experiments demonstrate that it fails on multi-step agentic coding tasks. In this paper, we explore this phenomenon and discuss possible factors contributing to this failure.
2605.11051.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基於的軟體工程代理面臨一個關鍵瓶頸：上下文長度的限制導致在複雜的長期任務上失敗。<lb/>一個有前景的解決方案是將上下文編碼為連續的嵌入，而不是離散的標記，這樣可以實現更密集的信息存儲。<lb/>我們為此目的應用了最近提出的上下文自編碼器。<lb/>雖然該方法在單次的常識和代碼理解任務上表現良好，但我們的實驗顯示它在多步的代理編碼任務上失敗。<lb/>在本文中，我們探討這一現象並討論可能導致此失敗的因素。
2605.11051.author: Kirill Gelvan et.al.
2605.11051.authors: Kirill Gelvan, Igor Slinko, Felix Steinbauer, Egor Bogomolov, Florian Kofler, Yaroslav Zharov
